text: This autofix PR addresses the authentication-authorization audit findings from issue #126. 8 of the 10 listed issues are properly fixed with correct gating (NODE_ENV checks, cookie fallback, public route synchronization, dead code removal). The most impactful fixes are the critical OTP bypass gating and the auth middleware cookie fallback. One remaining issue (devOtp storage in verify-otp handleResend) and one untouched audit finding (memoryToken reset) persist.

reasoning: One critical-pattern issue remains unfixed (verify-otp handleResend unconditionally stores devOtp to sessionStorage) and one minor audit finding (memoryToken refresh latency) is unaddressed. All other fixes are correct and well-gated.

message: Cookie fallback in requireAuth elegantly solves the page-refresh 401 cycle. Prioritizes Bearer header (existing behavior) then falls back to the HttpOnly access_token cookie — minimal change, maximum impact.

OTP bypass is correctly gated behind both NODE_ENV !== 'production' and ALLOW_OTP_BYPASS === 'true'. This avoids production exposure while preserving e2e test capability — much better than the original unconditional bypass.
Skipping /users/me on public routes via enabled: !isPublicRoute(pathname) is a clean, efficient approach that eliminates unnecessary 401-refresh cycles on login/register pages.

handleResend stores devOtp to sessionStorage unconditionally without a NEXT_PUBLIC_DEV_OTP guard. Same class of issue as the fixed login (line 61) and register (line 269) pages — an attacker with DevTools access can read the OTP from sessionStorage even in production.

Audit finding: memoryToken reset on page refresh triggers a guaranteed 401 before the refresh interceptor kicks in. This PR does not address it — the fix (sessionStorage or cookie fallback) was listed in the audit but not implemented.
New auto-generated file with empty state arrays. If this is a side-effect of the review tooling, it should be gitignored or removed from the PR diff to avoid file bloat.
